chapter_context.summary: Contrasts the heavy, burdensome idols of Babylon (Bel and Nebo) that must be carried, with the true God who carries and delivers His people from birth to old age.

outline.text: Babylon's Idols Carried vs. God Who Carries Israel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Babylon's Idols Carried vs. God Who Carries Israel (46:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Rebuking Idolatry; Declaring the End from the Beginning
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rebuking Idolatry; Declaring the End from the Beginning (46:5-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Comfort and Redemption
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of comfort and redemption, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 46, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
